channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Why Iranians are taking to Tehran’s streets during war
item.description: As bombs fall and millions are displaced, protests grow in Iran. Could this war be strengthening Iran’s resolve?

item.title: Drones expand the deadly front lines of Sudan’s devastating war
item.description: The United Nations is warning that escalating drone warfare in Sudan has killed more than 200 people in over a week.

item.title: What the Iran war looks like from the occupied West Bank
item.description: Iran missile shrapnel falls on West Bank Palestinians as settler attacks, Israeli raids and restrictions continue.

item.title: Why aren’t gold prices rising, despite Iran war uncertainty?
item.description: Gold holds steady despite war and rising oil prices, as recent volatility and a strong dollar reduce its appeal.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may amount to war crimes, UN rights office says
item.description: 'Deliberately attacking civilians, civilian objects amounts to war crime,' UN says after resurgence in Israeli strikes.

item.title: Rescue teams recover bodies after deadly Kabul hospital air strike
item.description: Afghan official condemns Pakistan air strike, calling it a crime against humanity targeting civilians and addicts.

item.title: Who is Gholamreza Soleimani, the commander of Iran’s Basij forces?
item.description: Israel claims to have killed Soleimani. Here is what we know about the veteran commander.

item.title: US National Counterterrorism Center director Joe Kent resigns over Iran war
item.description: Kent, who advised Trump and national intelligence director on 'terror' threats, says Iran 'posed no imminent threat'.

item.title: US envoys meet Hamas in Cairo to salvage fragile Gaza truce
item.description: Rare US-Hamas talks come as the war on Iran strains the battered Gaza 'ceasefire' and post-war plans.

item.title: Gavaskar links Sunrisers signing of Pakistan’s Abrar to Indian deaths
item.description: The signing of Pakistan bowler Abrar Ahmed by Hundred franchise Sunrisers bucked trend by Indian-owned teams.
